title: potentilla arenaria f. minutula
intro: Potentilla arenaria f. minutula, also known as the small creeping cinquefoil, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the rose family (Rosaceae). Traditionally, it has been used in herbal medicine due to its purported medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as soothing digestive issues, reducing fever, and alleviating symptoms of skin conditions like eczema. Potentilla arenaria f. minutula can be used in herbal preparations like teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often made by infusing the dried flowers and leaves in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
